phase1.output:
Age: 25
Price: $19.99
Grade: A
Is Java fun? true
phase1.explanation:
Java has 8 primitive data types. We're using 4 here:

int — whole numbers (no decimals)
double — decimal numbers
char — a single character in single quotes ''
boolean — true or false

Variable declaration: type name = value;

Naming rules:
• Can start with letter, underscore _, or dollar sign $
• Can't start with a number
• Can't use reserved keywords (int, class, etc.)
• Use camelCase: myVariableName

Sout tip: System.out.println can combine strings with variables using the + operator!
phase1.openingDialogue: Variables are like labeled boxes — you put values in them and give them names! 📦

phase2.errorMessage:
DataTypesExample.java:5: error: incompatible types: String cannot be converted to char
        char grade = "A";
                     ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
char uses SINGLE quotes 'A' — double quotes "A" is for String (text). This is a classic beginner error!

Java is strongly typed — you can't assign a String to a char variable. The compiler caught this and told you exactly what went wrong.

phase3.prompt:
Create a program with variables for:
• A student's age (int) — set to 20
• Their GPA (double) — set to 3.75
• Their first initial (char) — set to 'J'
• Whether they're enrolled (boolean) — set to true

Print all 4 values with descriptive labels.
dialogueHints: You need 4 variables — one of each type mentioned.
Make sure char uses single quotes: 'J' not "J".
Print them using System.out.println with descriptive labels.
phase3.openingDialogue: Alright, show me you can handle these variables~ 🎯
